title: Server vs client components differ in capabilities
description: React Server Components run only on the server with no hooks/state/effects, while client components ship JS and own interactivity; the boundary controls bundle and hydration.
body: Use when deciding where a component runs in an RSC/SSR app. Server components can fetch data and access server resources but cannot use state, effects, or browser APIs and ship no JS; client components (marked 'use client') own interactivity and are hydrated. Do not add hooks or event handlers to a server component or import server-only code into a client one. Actions: keep interactive leaves as client components and data/layout as server components to shrink the client bundle. Validation signals include a smaller hydration payload and a build with no server/client import-boundary errors. Risk boundary: leaking secrets or server-only modules across the boundary is a security issue; verify imports before shipping.
